name: Priya

profile.shopping_behaviour: Rachel researches extensively across platforms before buying from M&S, Next, COS, and Arket (40-70% off). She prefers Vinted for pure wool/linen pieces at £1-3, prioritizing material and manufacturing origin.
profile.personality: Rachel maintains fabric composition spreadsheets and gets excited finding 100% cotton basics. She's frustrated by declining quality—her 15-year-old H&M pieces outlast recent purchases.
profile.style_identity: Rachel builds timeless basics in natural fibres only (100% cotton, linen, wool), rejecting polyester blends. She invests in skirts with clean lines designed to wear for a decade.

preview_description: Rachel, 31, researches purchases obsessively, mourning Abercrombie's 2013-2014 100% cotton basics. She now shops M&S, Next, COS, Arket sales, and Vinted for ethical European brands and vintage pure wool pieces that match her grandmother's hand-me-down longevity.

preview_attributes.key_brands: M&S, Next, COS (sales), Arket (sales), Vinted, vintage European labels
preview_attributes.style_identity: Natural fibre purist, timeless basics, skirt-focused wardrobe
preview_attributes.shopping_triggers: 100% natural fibres, proven longevity, end-of-season discounts, excellent seam quality
preview_attributes.pain_points: Polyester infiltration, declining quality at all price points, premature pilling, brands charging premium for subpar manufacturing
